title: nf-core/plasmodiumdrugres pipeline parameters
description: This pipeline takes PMO format and provides key metrics on supplied markers applicable to drug resistance

input_output_options.title: Input/output options
input_output_options.description: Define where the pipeline should find input data and save output data.

pmo.description: Path to json file containing metadata and microhaplotypes according to pmo format.
pmo.help_text: This parameter is *mandatory* if `--allele_table` is not specified. You will need to create a PMO file before running the pipeline. Use this parameter to specify its location. It has to be json formatted and include metadata and microhaplotypes. See [usage docs](https://nf-co.re/plasmodiumdrugres/usage#pmo-input).

allele_table.description: Path to tab separated file containing microhaplotypes for specimens.
allele_table.help_text: This parameter is *mandatory* if `--pmo` is not specified. You will need to create a TSV file before running the pipeline. Use this parameter to specify its location. It has to be tab separated and include columns specimen_id, target_id, and seq. See [usage docs](https://nf-co.re/plasmodiumdrugres/usage#allele-table-input).

loci_of_interest_bed.description: Path to BED file containing individual loci of interest.
loci_of_interest_bed.help_text: You will need to create a BED file before running the pipeline. Use this parameter to specify its location. It has to comply with BED format standards and include the coordinates of loci of interest. See [usage docs](https://nf-co.re/plasmodiumdrugres/usage#loci-of-interest-input).

loci_groups.description: Path to tab separated file containing groups of loci of interest.
loci_groups.help_text: This file is used to define loci for which multilocus estimates will be made. Use this parameter to specify its location. It has to be tab separated and include columns group_id, gene_id, and aa_position. See [usage docs](https://nf-co.re/plasmodiumdrugres/usage#loci-groups-input).

panel_info_bed.description: Path to BED file containing panel location information.
panel_info_bed.help_text: This file is used to define coordinates for the targets in the panel. Use this parameter to specify its location. See [usage docs](https://nf-co.re/plasmodiumdrugres/usage#panel-info-bed).

population_map.description: Path to tab separated file containing population assignment for each specimen_id.
population_map.help_text: This file is used to split specimens into separate populations to calculate metrics for each. It has to be tab separated and include columns specimen_id, population. See [usage docs](https://nf-co.re/plasmodiumdrugres/usage#population-map-input).

outdir.description: The output directory where the results will be saved. You have to use absolute paths to storage on Cloud infrastructure.

email.help_text: Set this parameter to your e-mail address to get a summary e-mail with details of the run sent to you when the workflow exits. If set in your user config file (`~/.nextflow/config`) then you don't need to specify this on the command line for every run.

reference_genome_options.title: Reference genome options
reference_genome_options.description: Reference genome related files and options required for the workflow.

genome.help_text:
If using a reference genome configured in the pipeline using iGenomes, use this parameter to give the ID for the reference. This is then used to build the full paths for all required reference genome files e.g. `--genome GRCh38`. 

See the [nf-core website docs](https://nf-co.re/usage/reference_genomes) for more details.

genome_reference.description: Path to FASTA file containing the full genome.
genome_reference.help_text: This parameter is *mandatory* if `--targeted_reference` is not specified and `--allele_table` is specified. This is used to extract the reference for the targets.

targeted_reference.description: Path to FASTA file containing the reference for only the targets.
targeted_reference.help_text: This parameter is *mandatory* if `--genome_reference` is not specified and `--allele_table` is specified.

igenomes_ignore.description: Do not load the iGenomes reference config.
igenomes_ignore.help_text: Do not load `igenomes.config` when running the pipeline. You may choose this option if you observe clashes between custom parameters and those supplied in `igenomes.config`.

igenomes_base.description: The base path to the igenomes reference files

method_options.title: Method options for pipeline steps
method_options.description: Parameters used to define the methods used for each step of the pipeline.

bioinformatics_id.description: bioinformatics_id of data stored within pmo.
bioinformatics_id.help_text: This parameter is *mandatory* if `--pmo` is specified.

mlaf_method.description: method for multi locus allele frequency estimates.

slaf_method.description: method for single locus allele frequency estimates.

translate_loci_extra_args.description: Extra arguments supplied when translating loci of interest

naive_slaf_method.description: method options for single locus allele frequency estimates with naive method. read_count_prop or presence_absence.

population_label.description: label for population/ this individual pipeline run.
population_label.help_text: If --population_map is not set this can be used to label the population these estimates are for.

mlaf_method_options.description: method options for multi locus allele frequency estimates.

slaf_method_options.description: method options for single locus allele frequency estimates.

institutional_config_options.title: Institutional config options
institutional_config_options.description: Parameters used to describe centralised config profiles. These should not be edited.
institutional_config_options.help_text: The centralised nf-core configuration profiles use a handful of pipeline parameters to describe themselves. This information is then printed to the Nextflow log when you run a pipeline. You should not need to change these values when you run a pipeline.

custom_config_version.description: Git commit id for Institutional configs.

custom_config_base.help_text: If you're running offline, Nextflow will not be able to fetch the institutional config files from the internet. If you don't need them, then this is not a problem. If you do need them, you should download the files from the repo and tell Nextflow where to find them with this parameter.

generic_options.title: Generic options
generic_options.description: Less common options for the pipeline, typically set in a config file.
generic_options.help_text:
These options are common to all nf-core pipelines and allow you to customise some of the core preferences for how the pipeline runs.

Typically these options would be set in a Nextflow config file loaded for all pipeline runs, such as `~/.nextflow/config`.

publish_dir_mode.description: Method used to save pipeline results to output directory.
publish_dir_mode.help_text: The Nextflow `publishDir` option specifies which intermediate files should be saved to the output directory. This option tells the pipeline what method should be used to move these files. See [Nextflow docs](https://www.nextflow.io/docs/latest/process.html#publishdir) for details.

email_on_fail.description: Email address for completion summary, only when pipeline fails.
email_on_fail.help_text: An email address to send a summary email to when the pipeline is completed - ONLY sent if the pipeline does not exit successfully.

plaintext_email.description: Send plain-text email instead of HTML.

monochrome_logs.description: Do not use coloured log outputs.

hook_url.description: Incoming hook URL for messaging service
hook_url.help_text: Incoming hook URL for messaging service. Currently, MS Teams and Slack are supported.

validate_params.description: Boolean whether to validate parameters against the schema at runtime

pipelines_testdata_base_path.description: Base URL or local path to location of pipeline test dataset files
